response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with a strong testing culture and genuine low-latency systems work, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role: professional C++ experience amounts to a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and the last decade has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms. He shows no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle practice, or industrial-automation domain exposure, all of which are core to this position rather than advantageous extras.
strengths: Real high-performance, concurrent systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) on services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling, and a track record of mentoring via workshops and code katas
Broad polyglot background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with event-driven platform work (GISCollective)
concerns: C++/STL depth is minimal and a decade old; recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not the role's core toolchain
No industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or automation-protocol experience anywhere in the history
No Azure exposure (AWS instead) and an unexplained multi-month employment gap in 2020
